Class SingleRuleApplicationInfo

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.engine.SingleRuleApplicationInfo
public class SingleRuleApplicationInfo extends Object

Represents information about the result of a single rule application in the KeY verification system.

This class encapsulates whether a rule was applied successfully or not, along with relevant details such as the reason for failure (if applicable), the associated proof goal, and the applied rule. It is used in the context of symbolic execution and proof obligations to track the outcome of applying a specific rule.

Instances of this class are created to indicate the result of a rule application:

  • If the rule application is successful, a success message and the applied rule are stored.
  • If the rule application fails, an explanatory message and relevant details are stored.
See Also:
  • ProofGoal
  • RuleApp

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SingleRuleApplicationInfo(String message, @Nullable ProofGoal<?> nonCloseableGoal, @Nullable RuleApp appliedRuleApp)
    Constructs a new SingleRuleApplicationInfo for a failed rule application.
    SingleRuleApplicationInfo(@Nullable ProofGoal<?> mayCloseableGoal, @NonNull RuleApp appliedRuleApp)
    Constructs a new SingleRuleApplicationInfo for a successful rule application.

  • Constructor Details

    • SingleRuleApplicationInfo

      public SingleRuleApplicationInfo(@Nullable ProofGoal<?> mayCloseableGoal, @NonNull RuleApp appliedRuleApp)
      Constructs a new SingleRuleApplicationInfo for a successful rule application.
      Parameters:
      mayCloseableGoal - the proof goal that may be closed by this rule application.
      appliedRuleApp - the rule that was successfully applied.

    • SingleRuleApplicationInfo

      public SingleRuleApplicationInfo(String message, @Nullable ProofGoal<?> nonCloseableGoal, @Nullable RuleApp appliedRuleApp)
      Constructs a new SingleRuleApplicationInfo for a failed rule application.
      Parameters:
      message - a message explaining why the rule application failed.
      nonCloseableGoal - the proof goal that could not be closed by this rule application.
      appliedRuleApp - the rule that was applied (if any).

  • Method Details

    • isSuccess

      public boolean isSuccess()
      Checks whether the rule application was successful.
      Returns:
      true if the rule application was successful, false otherwise.

    • getGoal

      public <G extends @Nullable ProofGoal<G>> G getGoal()
      Retrieves the proof goal associated with this rule application.
      Type Parameters:
      G - the type of the proof goal.
      Returns:
      the proof goal associated with this rule application.

    • message

      public String message()

      Retrieves the message explaining the result of the rule application.

      For successful applications, this will typically be a success message. For unsuccessful applications, it will explain why the rule could not be applied.

      Returns:
      the result message of the rule application.

    • getAppliedRuleApp

      public @Nullable RuleApp getAppliedRuleApp()
      Retrieves the rule that was applied.
      Returns:
      the applied rule, or null if no rule was applied.
